purpose: Explains why central banks cannot simultaneously achieve inflation control, financial stability, and sovereign funding sustainability when debt is high
components: Objective 1: Inflation control (requires higher rates when inflation above target)
Objective 2: Financial stability (requires accommodative conditions when leveraged positions stressed)
Objective 3: Sovereign funding (requires manageable debt service costs)
Self-referential channel: rate hikes generate fiscal impulse offsetting 10-25% of contractionary intent
Credibility hierarchy: Fed > ECB > BoJ based on episode validation
current_assessment: The impairment is real but produces decades of muddling through rather than dramatic fracture. Central banks will oscillate between objectives, occasionally sacrificing one for another, leading to persistent moderate inflation and intermittent financial stress events.
limitations: Severity of impairment (5/10 confidence) much less certain than mechanism (7/10)
Multiple alternative explanations exist (post-pandemic shifts, supply-side inflation)
Claim that central bankers cannot publicly acknowledge fiscal constraints is unfalsifiable
N=1 tightening cycle in high-debt environment provides limited empirical basis
